article_title: First Targeted Therapy for Dermatomyositis Succeeds in Landmark 52-Week Trial, Published in NEJM With FDA Priority Review

content_preview.title: First Targeted Therapy for Dermatomyositis Succeeds in Landmark 52-Week Trial, Published in NEJM With FDA Priority Review
content_preview.summary: Brepocitinib, a first-in-class TYK2/JAK1 inhibitor, hit all primary and secondary endpoints in the largest-ever dermatomyositis trial, offering the first steroid-sparing treatment for a disease that has lacked targeted therapy for decades.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The New England Journal of Medicine published results on March 28 from the Phase 3 VALOR trial showing that brepocitinib, a first-in-class oral TYK2/JAK1 inhibitor, achieved statistically significant improvements across every primary and secondary endpoint in adults with dermatomyositis, [according to Priovant Therapeutics](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/03/28/3264202/34323/en/New-England-Journal-of-Medicine-Publishes-Positive-Phase-3-VALOR-Trial-Results-of-Brepocit...

editor_notes.source_verification: GlobeNewsWire confirmed brepocitinib, VALOR Phase 3, 241 patients, NEJM, FDA Priority Review, all endpoints met.
